title: Foster Parent/Family Recruitment - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > Foster Parent/Family Recruitment 62 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Developmental Issues Native American Community 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS AID FOSTER CARE AND ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program Information Coordinator (PIC) provides information to families interested in foster care or adoption. Staff guides families through the certification and home study process for foster care and adoption programs. Foster parents receive ongoing training to maintain certification and support. 9320 SW Barbur Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Foster parents must be age 21 or older and complete necessary training and application Hours: Family support: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Information sessions: -Infant adoption: 1st Tuesday of most months -Foster care adoption: 2nd Tuesday of the month Email (503) 222-9661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT COOS COUNTY OFFICE FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Foster care homes for people with developmental disabilities. Homes provide residential placement, 24-hour supervision, and assistance with daily living activities. Agency also recruits foster care providers for people with disabilities. 3030 Broadway Suite A North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Adults with developmental disabilities; call for eligibility requirements to become a foster care provider Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 226-7300 10 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT CURRY COUNTY OFFICE FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Foster care homes for people with developmental disabilities. Homes provide residential placement, 24-hour supervision, and assistance with daily living activities. Agency also recruits foster care providers for people with disabilities. 603 Hemlock Street Suite 3A Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Adults with developmental disabilities; call for eligibility requirements to become a foster care provider Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 226-7300 10 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT DOUGLAS COUNTY OFFICE FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Foster care homes for people with developmental disabilities. Homes provide residential placement, 24-hour supervision, and assistance with daily living activities. Agency also recruits foster care providers for people with disabilities. 2550 NW Edenbower Boulevard Suite 104 Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Adults with developmental disabilities; call for eligibility requirements to become a foster care provider Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 315-4300 205 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT JOSEPHINE COUNTY OFFICE FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Foster care homes for people with developmental disabilities. Homes provide residential placement, 24-hour supervision, and assistance with daily living activities. Agency also recruits foster care providers for people with disabilities. 1215 NE 7th Street Suite C Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Adults with developmental disabilities; call for eligibility requirements to become a foster care provider Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 474-6072 318 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT COMMUNITY LIVING CASE MANAGEMENT WASCO, SHERMAN, HOOD RIVER COUNTY OFFICE FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Foster care homes for people with developmental disabilities. Homes provide residential placement, 24-hour supervision, and assistance with daily living activities. Agency also recruits foster care providers for people with disabilities. 207 West 3rd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Adults with developmental disabilities; call for eligibility requirements to become a foster care provider Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 716-0135 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ELYSIUM SANCTUARY GUARDIAN CIRCLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Resources, outreach, and training connections are provided for those interested in becoming a resource parent, respite provider, or babysitter. Offers virtual support groups focused on sharing and self-care. Confidential Mailing Address Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details FOSTERCLUB FOSTER PARENT ONLINE TRAINING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The online training is a subscription-based service offering interactive educational modules for credit to resource parents, supportive adults, and child welfare professionals. To confirm if earned credits are accepted, contact your local agency. 620 S Holladay Drive #1 Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Resource parents, supportive adults and/or child welfare professionals Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 717-1552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KAIROS KAIROS JACKSON SERVICES TREATMENT FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits, selects, and trains treatment foster parents (TFP) from the local community and matches them with youth needing a therapeutic foster home setting. Also provides Behavior Rehabilitation Services (BRS) level treatment to youth who meet referral criteria and are matched with an available TFP. 10 Crater Lake Avenue Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Ages 4-17 who are under the jurisdiction of ODHS Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 772-0127 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KAIROS KAIROS MOMENTUM RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOME TREATMENT FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits, selects, and trains treatment foster parents (TFP) from the local community and matches them with youth needing a therapeutic foster home setting. Also provides Behavior Rehabilitation Services (BRS) level treatment to youth who meet referral criteria and are matched with an available TFP. 1920 Kurtz Lane Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Ages 4-17 who are under the jurisdiction of ODHS Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 295-3072 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES PLANNED AND RESPITE CARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short term respite services. Recruits and certifies respite providers for the program. 11035 NE Sandy Boulevard Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: To be a resource respite certified home, the following basic requirements must be met: -At least 21 years old -Can pass a background check -Can demonstrate financial independence (families cannot be dependent upon the foster care stipend) -Complete orientation and pre-certification training, and maintain certification through continued education and training -Reliable transportation -At least one driver over the age of 21 with a valid Oregon driver’s license -Separate bedroom with a door and a window for the foster kid placed in your home Requirements for youth to be placed in a respite home are as follows: -Ages 3-17 -Reside in Multnomah, Clackamas, and Wasington Counties -Active case, respite included in the treatment plan, and referred by the mental health therapist -Have Oregon Health Plan (OHP) coverage (not open card) -Approved by CareOregon with a respite authorization -At risk for out-of-home placement due to their mental health diagnosis -Have behaviors that the service can stabilize -The emotional distress of the family, caused by the youth’s mental health disorder, can be decreased with the service -Not actively suicidal, homicidal, or aggressive -Actively involved with a mental health provider who will provide after-hours crisis intervention during the respite period -Have a placement in a foster home or their family home is available at the end of the respite stay Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (855) 838-4113 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER FOSTER CARE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps Native youth and families involved with foster care by offering cultural support, connecting them to community resources, and assisting with reunification or finding permanent homes. Services include group meetings, support classes, and family events. Also recruits and supports Native American foster families, guiding them through the certification process. Services for foster parents include Positive Indian Parenting classes, Sibling and Family Night events, and help with genealogy and regalia. Does not provide legal advice. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Native American youth ages 0-24 who are in state or tribal foster care systems, their birth parents, and their foster parents. Young adults who have aged out of foster care can receive services through age 24. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF BAKER, UNION, AND WALLOWA COUNTIES BAKER CITY CHILD WELFARE OFFICE CHILD WELFARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help in keeping families united. Finds temporary and permanent homes for kids who need them, including foster and adoptive homes. The program also teaches people how to become resource parents. Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed on major holidays (541) 523-6423 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF BAKER, UNION, AND WALLOWA COUNTIES BAKER CITY CHILD WELFARE OFFICE FOSTER, ADOPT, AND KINSHIP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits, trains, and supports foster and kinship caregivers. Provides resources and connections, including support groups for resource providers, adoptive parents, and kinship caregivers. Provides information for adopting and/or fostering children that are currently in state care and DHS custody only. 500 Summer Street NE Suite E67 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Individuals who are interested in becoming resource parents or adopting a child that is being cared for by the state Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (800) 331-0503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON YOUTH AUTHORITY FOSTER PROCTOR HOMES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF An Oregon Youth Authority (OYA) Proctor Parent provides a safe, structured home for youth (typically 12-24) in OYA's care due to legal issues. These youth often have significant behavioral or emotional challenges. Proctor parents work with OYA and agencies to help them develop skills and achieve successful independence. 530 Center Street NE Suite 500 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: To become an Oregon Youth Authority (OYA) Proctor Parent: - Be at least 21 years old and a U.S. citizen or permanent resident - Pass a criminal background check and a home study/inspection -Complete required training -Emotionally mature -Financially stable. -Demonstrate a strong commitment to helping youth -Willingness to collaborate with OYA and other professionals Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email
